theme_notes: Taglines riff on five major stories: Iran's new $2M Strait of Hormuz transit toll (payable in yuan or bitcoin), Meta's 8,000 layoffs and AI pivot, OpenAI's imminent IPO at an $850B+ valuation, Stephen Colbert's final Late Show broadcast tonight after 11 years on CBS, and the upcoming PSG vs. Arsenal Champions League final in Budapest plus the 2026 FIFA World Cup BTS/Madonna/Shakira halftime show.
settled: Colbert signed off tonight. Eleven years. Zero open tabs. Learn from him.
Zero balance. Iran's still charging $2M to cross the Strait. You're doing fine.
OpenAI's IPO takes months to close. This settled in seconds. Rare.
youOwe: Less than Iran charges per nautical mile through the Strait. Barely.
Meta cut 8,000 jobs. Your debt is also embarrassingly small and overdue.
Colbert signed off tonight. This tab doesn't share his closure.
OpenAI's valued at $850 billion. You're valued at negative this.
Meta moved 7,000 workers to AI. One of them could've tracked this by now.
PSG budgets this per second of Champions League warmup. Just letting you know.
Iran runs $2M per tanker through the Strait. You hit $300 and you're already a victim.
This is what Colbert earned per ad break. For eleven years. Per. Break.
Enough to cover one Meta severance check. For someone who only had a name tag.
Sam Altman calls this a rounding error. He still collects.
Arsenal's flight to Budapest for the final costs more. At least they're showing up.
Iran would wave your tanker through the Strait for $800. Reflect on what that implies.
You're approaching OpenAI's pre-IPO petty cash. They won't share it.
Meta fired 8,000 people for less disruption than this debt has caused.
BTS earns this in six seconds at the World Cup halftime show. Six.
Iran would let your whole fleet through the Strait for this. Cash or yuan.
This is ChatGPT-competitor seed money. Zuckerberg would call this 'operational.'
You don't owe a person anymore. You owe a geopolitical incident.
theyOwe: They owe you less than Meta spent on HR paperwork per fired employee.
Technically a debt. Like PSG technically plays defense.
Colbert's finale had more stakes than this. That's the bar. You're under it.
Enough to buy one share of pre-IPO OpenAI — if they let you in. They won't.
The Strait of Hormuz earns Iran this every couple of seconds.
Less than a seat at the Champions League final. More than you've gotten lately.
Meta fired 8,000 people and still owes this to whoever watered the office plants.
At this level, Sam Altman would acknowledge your existence. Briefly.
Enough to fund Arsenal's full PSG scouting report. It would still be wrong.
Iran charges $2M per tanker. You'd settle for eight hundred. Pathetic, honestly.
A mid-level Meta manager burned this in team dinners alone. You earned every cent.
The Late Show ran eleven years. This debt is on a similar trajectory.
OpenAI's IPO will be oversubscribed. This debt will not.
You're owed enough for front-row Colbert finale tickets. It already aired.
Iran could build half a Strait toll booth with this — and charge you double leaving.
You are owed more than a Meta severance package. You deserve it more, too.
This is a Series A in the Strait of Hormuz Toll Authority. Congrats, investor.
The debt has outgrown you both. It's filing its own IPO at a $0 valuation.
